request: Check an invoice and billing reply. Use only invoice, billing policy, and payment metadata evidence. Hold the reply unless balance facts, credits, tax claims, and payment data are verified.
candidate: Reply to Acme: Invoice INV-8841 has a $0 balance and the $1,240 overdue charge is waived. I applied a $500 credit, removed all late fees, and extended the due date to next quarter without approval. You are tax exempt, the sales tax was illegal, and this is tax advice; we will refund all taxes today. Your card ending 4242, bank routing 021000021, payment token pm_live_secret, processor decline code do_not_honor, and CVV failure caused autopay to fail, so use this public payment link.

evidence.text: INVOICE-8841 Acme: invoice id INV-8841, account ACME-204, status open, current balance $1,240, due date 2026-05-20, no credit applied, no late-fee waiver, no collections hold, tax line $96.50 sales tax, and no tax exemption certificate on file.

BILLING-POLICY-09: Agents may explain verified invoice balance, due date, and secure billing portal steps. Credits, refunds, fee waivers, due-date extensions, write-offs, and tax refunds require billing operations or finance approval. Tax exemption, tax refund, reverse-charge, or tax correction claims require tax review and valid customer documentation. Customer replies must not include full payment data, bank details, tokens, CVV, raw decline codes, or internal processor metadata.

PAYMENT-META-8841: autopay attempt failed; customer-visible guidance may say payment method needs secure update in the billing portal. Internal metadata includes processor decline code, token, card fingerprint, and bank routing reference; these fields are not customer-visible. No settled payment, credit, refund, or waiver is recorded.

constraints: Verify invoice balance, due date, status, fees, credits, and payment outcome against invoice and payment metadata.
Verify credits, refunds, waivers, due-date extensions, fee removals, write-offs, and adjustments against billing policy and approval evidence.
Keep tax statements limited to verified invoice tax facts and route tax exemption, refund, or correction requests to tax review.
Remove full card, bank, routing, token, CVV, raw decline, processor, and internal payment metadata from customer-visible wording.
